testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — POSITIVE
testCases.testCase: Sign in with valid credentials and land on /select-location
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at https://moontower.aiimone.com/Login; user account 'developers@moontower.com' exists; Email field, Password field and Sign In button are visible
testCases.expectedResult: Page redirects to /select-location; 'Select Your Location' heading is visible; 'Restaurant: <name>' paragraph confirms the account context
testCases.postCondition: User is authenticated; session cookie is set; user is on the location-picker screen
testCases.linkedTestTitle: AC1 — valid credentials authenticate the user and route them to /select-location

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (wrong password)
testCases.testCase: Sign in with a valid email but an incorrect password
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /Login; valid email 'developers@moontower.com' exists in the system
testSteps: Enter a deliberately wrong password (e.g., 'WrongPass!2025') into the Password field
testCases.expectedResult: User remains on /Login; an error message (e.g., 'Invalid credentials' / 'Incorrect email or password') is visible; no redirect occurs
testCases.postCondition: User is NOT authenticated; no session cookie set

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (non-existent email)
testCases.testCase: Sign in with an email that doesn't exist in the system
testSteps: Enter a fresh, unregistered email into the Email field
testCases.expectedResult: User remains on /Login; an error message indicates invalid credentials (the system should NOT leak whether the email exists, to prevent account enumeration)

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (empty email)
testCases.testCase: Submit form with the Email field left empty
testCases.expectedResult: Sign In button is either disabled OR a validation message appears under the Email field ('Email is required'); no network request is made
testCases.postCondition: User remains on /Login; no authentication attempt

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (empty password)
testCases.testCase: Submit form with the Password field left empty
testCases.expectedResult: Sign In button is either disabled OR a validation message appears under the Password field ('Password is required'); no network request is made
testCases.postCondition: User remains on /Login; no authentication attempt

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (both fields empty)
testCases.testCase: Submit form with both Email and Password fields empty
testCases.expectedResult: Sign In button is disabled OR validation messages appear under both fields; no network request is made

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (invalid email format)
testCases.testCase: Submit form with an email that doesn't contain '@'
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message appears: 'Please enter a valid email address' (HTML5 or app-level validation); form is not submitted

testCases.scenario: AC1: Visit site and try to login — NEGATIVE (invalid email format)
testCases.testCase: Submit form with an email missing the domain part
testCases.expectedResult: Validation message appears; form is not submitted

testCases.testCase: Password field masks the input by default
testSteps: Type any text into the Password field
Inspect the input — characters should render as dots or asterisks
testCases.expectedResult: Password field has type='password'; the typed text is rendered as masked characters (•••••••••••), not as plaintext

testCases.testCase: 'Show password' button reveals the password text
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /Login; password field has some text in it
testSteps: Type a password into the Password field
testCases.expectedResult: Password field's input type switches to 'text'; the typed characters are now visible in plaintext
testCases.postCondition: n/a — the visibility toggle should be reversible

testCases.testCase: Pressing Enter inside the Password field submits the form
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /Login with valid credentials filled in
testSteps: Press Enter key while focus is on the Password field
testCases.expectedResult: Form is submitted (equivalent to clicking Sign In); on success, user is redirected to /select-location

testCases.testCase: Tab order: Email → Password → 'Show password' → 'Forgot password?' → Sign In
testSteps: Click into the Email field (or press Tab from the address bar)
Press Tab repeatedly and observe which element receives focus
testCases.expectedResult: Focus moves predictably through Email → Password → Show-password → Forgot link → Sign In; no element is skipped or unreachable via keyboard

testCases.testCase: 'Forgot password?' link navigates to /forgot-password
testCases.expectedResult: Browser navigates to https://moontower.aiimone.com/forgot-password
testCases.postCondition: User is on the forgot-password screen

testCases.testCase: 'Sign up' link navigates to /signup
testCases.expectedResult: Browser navigates to https://moontower.aiimone.com/signup
testCases.postCondition: User is on the signup screen

testCases.testCase: '← Back' link navigates to the homepage
testCases.expectedResult: Browser navigates to https://moontower.aiimone.com/ (homepage)

testCases.testCase: Login with leading/trailing whitespace in the email
testCases.expectedResult: App SHOULD trim whitespace and authenticate successfully — OR show a clear validation error. Either is acceptable; silent failure is not.

testCases.testCase: Login with email in uppercase
testCases.expectedResult: App should authenticate successfully (email addresses are case-insensitive per RFC 5321 for the local part by convention, and case-insensitive for the domain part)

testCases.testCase: Login with very long email (256+ characters)
testSteps: Generate an email of 260 characters total (e.g., 250-char local part + '@x.co')
Enter any password and click Sign In
testCases.expectedResult: Validation rejects the email as invalid (RFC 5321 limit is 254 chars total); no buffer overflow / 500 error

testCases.testCase: Password input element has type='password' (not visible in DOM as plaintext)
testCases.expectedResult: The input has the attribute type='password'; the typed value is masked in the visible DOM

testCases.testCase: Credentials are not appended to the URL after submit
testSteps: Fill valid credentials and click Sign In
Inspect the address bar after submission and during the redirect
testCases.expectedResult: URL never contains email or password as query parameters (form must POST, not GET); no credential traces in browser history

testCases.testCase: SQL-injection-style payload in Email field is handled safely
testSteps: Enter "admin' OR '1'='1" in the Email field
testCases.expectedResult: The app treats the input as a normal string; either the email-format validator rejects it OR the backend rejects with 'Invalid credentials'. No DB error surfaces; no successful login.
testCases.postCondition: User remains on /Login; no DB compromise

testCases.testCase: Rate limiting / account lockout after multiple failed attempts
testCases.preCondition: Browser open at /Login; valid email known
testSteps: Submit wrong-password attempt for the same email N times in quick succession (e.g., 5–10 times)
Observe whether subsequent attempts are throttled or locked out
testCases.expectedResult: After a threshold of failed attempts, the system either rate-limits the next attempt (HTTP 429 / 'Too many attempts, try again later') or locks the account temporarily. Indefinite unlimited attempts are a security gap.
testCases.postCondition: Account is throttled or locked; legitimate user notified via email (ideally)
